title: Floating-point numbers should not be tested for equality
description: This rule checks for comparisons of floating-point numbers using equality operators, which can lead to inaccuracies due to floating-point precision issues.
message: Avoid comparing floating-point numbers using equality operators.
recommendation: Use a tolerance when comparing floating-point numbers or consider using an alternative data type if precision is critical.

noncompliant.description: Comparison of floating-point numbers using equality operator

compliant.description: Comparison of floating-point numbers with a tolerance

why_is_this_an_issue: Floating-point numbers have limited precision and can produce inaccurate results when compared using equality operators.
how_to_fix: Use a tolerance when comparing floating-point numbers or consider using an alternative data type if precision is critical.

logic.logic.new_check_type_doc: Checks if the two compared values are both of type 'float'

function_code:
def check_floating_point_equality(node):
    return (isinstance(node['left']['value'], float) and isinstance(node['right']['value'], float))

metadata.title: Floating-point numbers should not be tested for equality
